questions.question: According to the lesson, what is Minus-space?
questions.correct_answer: Minus-space is that which possesses neither extension nor position. It is not emptiness, but the absence of the container itself, the cancellation of the act of distinguishing 'here' and 'there.'

questions.question: What are the three types of spatial concepts described in the lesson?
questions.correct_answer: Space (which possesses extension and metric), Point or zero-space (which possesses position but not extension), and Minus-space (which possesses neither extension nor position).

questions.question: How does movement work in Minus-space according to the hypothesis?
questions.correct_answer: In Minus-space there is no distance, so transition is instantaneous. A particle can disappear and appear thousands of light-years away because distance was not overcome - it simply did not exist.

questions.question: What does the lesson suggest black holes might actually be?
questions.correct_answer: The lesson suggests that black holes might be portals or doors where matter breaks through the barrier of our reality and goes into Minus-space, rather than compressing into a singularity.

questions.question: What is the main limitation of the Minus-space hypothesis mentioned in the lesson?
questions.correct_answer: The hypothesis remains a philosophical concept with poetic metaphors rather than a scientific theory, because it lacks mathematical rigor and precise equations that can be verified.
